mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 4.5 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 60% of months where UNRATE exceeds 4.5 percent, or the signal shows zero predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts and real savings deposits indicate collapsing consumer demand and liquidity constraints, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline of HOUST and RSAFS fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.05 in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects robust manufacturing output that supports corporate earnings expectations, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns do not materialize in over 50% of late-cycle expansion periods when INDPRO increases, or the mechanism reverses direction during supply shocks.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate surpasses 5.0 percent, restrictive monetary policy stifles economic growth and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 65% of observations where FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0 percent, invalidating the restrictive policy penalty.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms strong labor market absorption, boosting consumer confidence and driving positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The combination of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields a negative or zero Rank IC over a 24-month rolling window, or fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product reflects sustained aggregate demand growth that translates into higher corporate revenues, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over three months.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward returns, or the signal produces negative alpha in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals persistent supply chain bottlenecks that delay earnings rebounds, causing negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns fail to materialize in over 55% of post-shock recovery periods when INDPRO falls below 110, or the signal loses predictive power after two consecutive quarters.
